_meta.produced_by: Claude (Anthropic) — MK Series AI layer build from DRS papers

paper_title: Meta-Kaizen in Networked Organizations: Governance Closure, Privacy Amplification, and Bayesian Calibration Under a Federated Architecture

phase_ready.note: PH-MK2.1: Exact constant C in Theorem 3.1 requires numerical evaluation. PH-MK2.2: No MKN has been implemented — all network demonstrations are structural simulations.

falsification_syntax: I-2 5-part (FALSIFIED IF / WHERE / EVALUATION / BOUNDARY / CONTEXT)
inference_rule_inventory: IR-1 through IR-8 — Fracttalix P2 Build Plan S48

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: Standard DP parameter choice — trade-off between privacy and utility. Not derived; operational default per Erlingsson et al. (2019).
Standard cryptographic convention for negligible failure probability. Not derived; operational default.
Derived from Theorem 3.1: substituting epsilon_local=3.0, delta=10^-5 into shuffle model amplification bound (Erlingsson et al. 2019; Feldman et al. 2021).

claim_registry.label: Shuffle model privacy amplification
claim_registry.name: Shuffle model privacy amplification
claim_registry.statement: The shuffle model (Erlingsson et al. 2019) provides central DP guarantees from local DP mechanisms: each participant applies local epsilon_local-DP randomizer, then submissions are uniformly permuted before delivery to the Aggregator. The central guarantee epsilon_central is strictly stronger than epsilon_local for sufficiently large n.

claim_registry.label: Club goods governance mechanism
claim_registry.name: Club goods governance mechanism
claim_registry.statement: Ostrom (1990) club goods mechanism creates symmetric incentives for honest participation in common-pool resource governance. Buchanan (1965) establishes the economic theory of clubs with excludable, non-rival membership benefits.

claim_registry.label: Beta-Binomial conjugacy
claim_registry.name: Beta-Binomial conjugacy
claim_registry.statement: The Beta distribution is conjugate to the Binomial likelihood: Beta(alpha, beta) prior with Binomial(n, p) likelihood yields Beta(alpha + successes, beta + failures) posterior. De Finetti (1937) exchangeability theorem justifies i.i.d. assumption under appropriate conditions.

claim_registry.label: Meta-Kaizen Network (MKN) architecture
claim_registry.name: Meta-Kaizen Network (MKN) architecture
claim_registry.statement: An MKN consists of: (1) Participant Organizations (POs) each running local Meta-Kaizen; (2) a Shuffle Proxy that permutes submissions; (3) an Aggregator that maintains the shared Knowledge Record Store; (4) a Master Decision Log for governance transparency.

claim_registry.label: Theorem 3.1 — Minimum Network Size
claim_registry.name: Theorem 3.1 — Minimum Network Size
claim_registry.statement: Under the shuffle model with epsilon_local=3.0 and delta=10^-5, the central DP guarantee satisfies epsilon_central = O((1 - e^(-epsilon_local)) * sqrt(e^(epsilon_local) * log(1/delta) / n)). Substituting parameters yields n >= C^2 * 208.8. At n=100, epsilon_central approximately 1.45; meaningful privacy (epsilon_central < 1.0) requires n approximately 200.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-1: Cited theorem application — Erlingsson et al. (2019) shuffle model amplification bound.
IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — substitute epsilon_local=3.0, delta=10^-5: (1-e^-3)=0.950; e^3=20.09; log(10^5)=11.51.
IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — evaluate at n=100 and n=200.
inference_rule_steps.output: n=100: epsilon_central approx 1.45; n=200: epsilon_central approx 1.02. Meaningful privacy requires n approx 200.

WHERE.epsilon_central_at_n200: scalar · dimensionless · central DP guarantee computed via Feldman et al. (2021) Theorem 3.2 with epsilon_local=3.0, delta=10^-5, n=200

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Compute epsilon_central using exact Feldman et al. (2021) formula (not asymptotic approximation) for n=200, epsilon_local=3.0, delta=10^-5; check result lies in [0.50, 2.0]; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: epsilon_central = 1.0 → NOT FALSIFIED (target threshold); epsilon_central = 2.0 → FALSIFIED (claimed 'meaningful' privacy not achieved at n=200)
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: The asymptotic approximation is used in the paper. Exact constant C requires numerical evaluation (PH-MK2.1). Feldman et al. (2021) formally requires n > 3923 for strict validity at epsilon_local=3.0.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {Feldman exact computation yields epsilon_central = 3.5 at n=200} → privacy not meaningful → FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.label: Theorem 7.1 — Temporal Consistency
claim_registry.name: Theorem 7.1 — Temporal Consistency
claim_registry.statement: The governance protocol is temporally isolated from improvements it governs through a cryptographic audit log. The framework cannot be retroactively modified to ratify previously evaluated improvements. Temporal separation holds if and only if the Master Decision Log is append-only and hash-chained.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-4: Definition Expansion — Master Decision Log defined as append-only, hash-chained ledger.
inference_rule_steps.output: Log entries are immutable once committed.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-1: Modus Ponens — if log is append-only and hash-chained, then any retroactive modification breaks the hash chain, which is detectable.
inference_rule_steps.output: Retroactive modification is detectable → temporal isolation holds.

WHERE.retroactive_modification_undetectable: boolean · true if an adversary can modify a prior log entry without breaking the hash chain or producing a detectable inconsistency, given SHA-256 or equivalent collision-resistant hash

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Attempt to modify a prior log entry in a hash-chained append-only log; verify whether the modification breaks the chain; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Hash collision found → FALSIFIED (but computationally infeasible for SHA-256)
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Temporal consistency relies on collision resistance of the hash function, a standard cryptographic assumption. The theorem is conditional on this assumption, not unconditional.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {SHA-256 collision found allowing undetectable log modification} → temporal consistency broken → FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.label: Theorem 7.2 — Governance Process-Equivalence
claim_registry.name: Theorem 7.2 — Governance Process-Equivalence
claim_registry.statement: The network governance protocol G and object-level improvement mechanism M share the same scoring function (KVS), threshold (kappa), and logging structure (Master Decision Log). The three institutional differences — principal set, ratification threshold, and review period — are parameterizations of the shared mechanism, not structural departures.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-4: Definition Expansion — governance protocol G uses KVS for scoring governance proposals; mechanism M uses KVS for scoring improvement candidates.
inference_rule_steps.output: Both use KVS, kappa, and MDL.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-6: Logical Equivalence — structural identity of scoring, thresholding, and logging implies process-equivalence up to parameterization.
inference_rule_steps.output: G and M are process-equivalent. Three differences are parameter choices.

WHERE.structural_departure_found: boolean · true if the governance protocol G uses a scoring function, threshold rule, or logging structure that is not a parameterization of the object-level mechanism M

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Compare G and M on three dimensions: (1) scoring function identity; (2) threshold rule identity; (3) logging structure identity. If any dimension shows a structural (not parametric) difference, structural_departure_found = true; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Parametric differences (different principal set, different kappa value, different review period) → NOT FALSIFIED. Structural difference (e.g., G uses a voting mechanism instead of KVS) → FALSIFIED.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Process-equivalence is weaker than process-identity. The paper explicitly characterizes three institutional differences as parameterizations. Claim is falsified only by a structural departure.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {Governance protocol uses majority vote instead of KVS scoring} → structural departure → FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.label: Bayesian calibration convergence
claim_registry.name: Bayesian calibration convergence
claim_registry.statement: Under the Beta-Binomial conjugate model with honest majority (C1), retrospective validation (C2), and bounded strategic benefit (C3), the network's calibrated threshold kappa_t converges to the true optimal threshold kappa* as the number of improvement cycles increases.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-1: Cited theorem application — Beta-Binomial conjugacy guarantees posterior concentration around true p as n increases (Bernstein-von Mises theorem).
IR-1: Modus Ponens — conditions C1-C3 jointly ensure exchangeability holds approximately.
inference_rule_steps.output: kappa_t → kappa* as improvement cycles accumulate.

WHERE.calibration_diverges: boolean · true if, under conditions C1-C3, the sequence kappa_t does not converge: |kappa_{t+1} - kappa_t| fails to decrease monotonically after t > 20 cycles

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Simulate Beta-Binomial updating under C1-C3 for 100 improvement cycles; compute |kappa_{t+1} - kappa_t| for t > 20; check for convergence (decreasing differences); finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Non-monotonic oscillation that still converges in L2 → NOT FALSIFIED (convergence does not require monotone approach)
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Convergence is conditional on C1-C3. Violation of exchangeability (e.g., correlated outcomes across substrates) can cause divergence. Three failure modes documented in MK-P2 Section 5.1.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {Coordinated strategic behavior by coalition of POs introduces systematic bias, causing kappa_t to oscillate without converging after 50 cycles} → C1 violated → conditional claim not falsified (condition violated, not conclusion).

placeholder_register.description: No MKN has been implemented. All network demonstrations are structural simulations. Empirical confirmation awaits actual network data.

cross_references.relation: Novelty measurement requires shared Knowledge Record Store — privacy-preserving sharing is the contribution of MK-P2.
Process-equivalence ensures governance changes pass through same KVS evaluation as object-level improvements.
Bayesian calibration provides the mechanism for threshold adaptation deferred in MK-P1.
